cat > .github/workflows/runner_determinator_script_sync.yaml << 'EOF'
name: runner-determinator

on:
  workflow_dispatch:
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]
    paths:
      - .github/workflows/_runner-determinator.yaml
      - .github/workflows/_runner_determinator_script_sync.yaml
      - .github/workflows/scripts/runner_determinator.py

jobs:
  python-script-sync-check:
    if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@11bd71901bbe5b1630ceea73d27597364c9af683 # v4.2.2
        with:
          sparse-checkout: |
            .github

      - name: Extract the script from runner_determinator
        run: |
            # Runner determinator files
            RUNNER_DETERMINATOR_WORKFLOW_FILE=.github/workflows/_runner-determinator.yml
            RUNNER_DETERMINATOR_PYTHON_SCRIPT_FILE=.github/scripts/runner_determinator.py

            # Parse the job file, extract the script and run it, up to the final EOF,
            # to generate the python file in the local folder
            yq '.jobs.runner-determinator.steps[] | select(.id == "hardcode-script") | .run' \
                "${RUNNER_DETERMINATOR_WORKFLOW_FILE}" | sed '/^EOF$/q' | bash

            set +e
            DIFF="$(diff "$(basename ${RUNNER_DETERMINATOR_PYTHON_SCRIPT_FILE})" ${RUNNER_DETERMINATOR_PYTHON_SCRIPT_FILE})"
            IS_DIFF=$?
            set -e
            if [ $IS_DIFF -eq 0 ]; then
                echo "Scripts are in sync! ^_^";
            else
                echo -e "Scripts are *NOT* in sync:\n ${DIFF}";
                exit 1
            fi
EOF

cat > .github/workflows/inductor-perf-test-nightly-rocm-mi355.yml << 'EOF'
name: inductor-perf-nightly-rocm-mi355

on:
  push:
    tags:
      - ciflow/inductor-perf-test-nightly-rocm-mi355/*
  schedule:
    - cron: 15 0 * * *
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      training:
        description: Run training (on by default)?
        required: false
        type: boolean
        default: true
      inference:
        description: Run inference (on by default)?
        required: false
        type: boolean
        default: true
      default:
        description: Run inductor_default?
        required: false
        type: boolean
        default: false
      dynamic:
        description: Run inductor_dynamic_shapes?
        required: false
        type: boolean
        default: false
      cppwrapper:
        description: Run inductor_cpp_wrapper?
        required: false
        type: boolean
        default: false
      cudagraphs:
        description: Run inductor_cudagraphs?
        required: false
        type: boolean
        default: true
      freezing_cudagraphs:
        description: Run inductor_cudagraphs with freezing for inference?
        required: false
        type: boolean
        default: false
      aotinductor:
        description: Run aot_inductor for inference?
        required: false
        type: boolean
        default: false
      maxautotune:
        description: Run inductor_max_autotune?
        required: false
        type: boolean
        default: false
      benchmark_configs:
        description: The list of configs used the benchmark
        required: false
        type: string
        default: inductor_huggingface_perf_rocm_mi355,inductor_timm_perf_rocm_mi355,inductor_torchbench_perf_rocm_mi355

concurrency:
  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.ref_name }}-${{ github.ref_type == 'branch' && github.sha }}-${{ github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' }}-${{ github.event_name == 'schedule' }}
  cancel-in-progress: true

permissions: read-all

jobs:
  get-label-type:
    name: get-label-type
    uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/workflows/_runner-determinator.yml@release/2.10
    if: ${{ (github.event_name != 'schedule' || github.repository == 'pytorch/pytorch') && github.repository_owner == 'pytorch' }}
    with:
      triggering_actor: ${{ github.triggering_actor }}
      issue_owner: ${{ github.event.pull_request.user.login || github.event.issue.user.login }}
      curr_branch: ${{ github.head_ref || github.ref_name }}
      curr_ref_type: ${{ github.ref_type }}
      opt_out_experiments: lf

  linux-jammy-rocm-py3_10-inductor-benchmark-build:
    if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'
    name: rocm-py3_10-inductor-benchmark-build
    uses: ./.github/workflows/_linux-build.yml
    with:
      build-environment: linux-jammy-rocm-py3_10
      docker-image-name: ci-image:pytorch-linux-jammy-rocm-n-py3-benchmarks
      test-matrix: |
        { include: [
          { config: "inductor_huggingface_perf_rocm_mi355", shard: 1, num_shards: 5, runner: "linux.rocm.gpu.mi355.1" },
          { config: "inductor_huggingface_perf_rocm_mi355", shard: 2, num_shards: 5, runner: "linux.rocm.gpu.mi355.1" },
          { config: "inductor_huggingface_perf_rocm_mi355", shard: 3, num_shards: 5, runner: "linux.rocm.gpu.mi355.1" },
          { config: "inductor_huggingface_perf_rocm_mi355", shard: 4, num_shards: 5, runner: "linux.rocm.gpu.mi355.1" },
          { config: "inductor_huggingface_perf_rocm_mi355", shard: 5, num_shards: 5, runner: "linux.rocm.gpu.mi355.1" },
          { config: "inductor_timm_perf_rocm_mi355", shard: 1, num_shards: 7, runner: "linux.rocm.gpu.mi355.1" },
          { config: "inductor_timm_perf_rocm_mi355", shard: 2, num_shards: 7, runner: "linux.rocm.gpu.mi355.1" },
          { config: "inductor_timm_perf_rocm_mi355", shard: 3, num_shards: 7, runner: "linux.rocm.gpu.mi355.1" },
          { config: "inductor_timm_perf_rocm_mi355", shard: 4, num_shards: 7, runner: "linux.rocm.gpu.mi355.1" },
          { config: "inductor_timm_perf_rocm_mi355", shard: 5, num_shards: 7, runner: "linux.rocm.gpu.mi355.1" },
          { config: "inductor_timm_perf_rocm_mi355", shard: 6, num_shards: 7, runner: "linux.rocm.gpu.mi355.1" },
          { config: "inductor_timm_perf_rocm_mi355", shard: 7, num_shards: 7, runner: "linux.rocm.gpu.mi355.1" },
          { config: "inductor_torchbench_perf_rocm_mi355", shard: 1, num_shards: 9, runner: "linux.rocm.gpu.mi355.1" },
          { config: "inductor_torchbench_perf_rocm_mi355", shard: 2, num_shards: 9, runner: "linux.rocm.gpu.mi355.1" },
          { config: "inductor_torchbench_perf_rocm_mi355", shard: 3, num_shards: 9, runner: "linux.rocm.gpu.mi355.1" },
          { config: "inductor_torchbench_perf_rocm_mi355", shard: 4, num_shards: 9, runner: "linux.rocm.gpu.mi355.1" },
          { config: "inductor_torchbench_perf_rocm_mi355", shard: 5, num_shards: 9, runner: "linux.rocm.gpu.mi355.1" },
          { config: "inductor_torchbench_perf_rocm_mi355", shard: 6, num_shards: 9, runner: "linux.rocm.gpu.mi355.1" },
          { config: "inductor_torchbench_perf_rocm_mi355", shard: 7, num_shards: 9, runner: "linux.rocm.gpu.mi355.1" },
          { config: "inductor_torchbench_perf_rocm_mi355", shard: 8, num_shards: 9, runner: "linux.rocm.gpu.mi355.1" },
          { config: "inductor_torchbench_perf_rocm_mi355", shard: 9, num_shards: 9, runner: "linux.rocm.gpu.mi355.1" },
        ]}
    secrets: inherit

  linux-jammy-rocm-py3_10-inductor-benchmark-test:
    permissions:
      id-token: write
      contents: read
    name: rocm-py3_10-inductor-benchmark-test
    uses: ./.github/workflows/_rocm-test.yml
    needs: linux-jammy-rocm-py3_10-inductor-benchmark-build
    with:
      build-environment: ${{ needs.linux-jammy-rocm-py3_10-inductor-benchmark-build.outputs.build-environment }}
      dashboard-tag: training-true-inference-true-default-true-dynamic-true-cudagraphs-true-cppwrapper-true-aotinductor-true-freezing_cudagraphs-true
      docker-image: ${{ needs.linux-jammy-rocm-py3_10-inductor-benchmark-build.outputs.docker-image }}
      test-matrix: ${{ needs.linux-jammy-rocm-py3_10-inductor-benchmark-build.outputs.test-matrix }}
      timeout-minutes: 720
      # Disable monitor in perf tests for more investigation
      disable-monitor: true
      monitor-log-interval: 10
      monitor-data-collect-interval: 2
    secrets: inherit
EOF

name: runner-determinator

on:
  workflow_dispatch:
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]
    paths:
      - .github/workflows/_runner-determinator.yaml
      - .github/workflows/_runner_determinator_script_sync.yaml
      - .github/workflows/scripts/runner_determinator.py

jobs:
  python-script-sync-check:
    if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@11bd71901bbe5b1630ceea73d27597364c9af683 # v4.2.2
        with:
          sparse-checkout: |
            .github

      - name: Extract the script from runner_determinator
        run: |
            # Runner determinator files
            RUNNER_DETERMINATOR_WORKFLOW_FILE=.github/workflows/_runner-determinator.yml
            RUNNER_DETERMINATOR_PYTHON_SCRIPT_FILE=.github/scripts/runner_determinator.py

            # Parse the job file, extract the script and run it, up to the final EOF,
            # to generate the python file in the local folder
            yq '.jobs.runner-determinator.steps[] | select(.id == "hardcode-script") | .run' \
                "${RUNNER_DETERMINATOR_WORKFLOW_FILE}" | sed '/^EOF$/q' | bash

            set +e
            DIFF="$(diff "$(basename ${RUNNER_DETERMINATOR_PYTHON_SCRIPT_FILE})" ${RUNNER_DETERMINATOR_PYTHON_SCRIPT_FILE})"
            IS_DIFF=$?
            set -e
            if [ $IS_DIFF -eq 0 ]; then
                echo "Scripts are in sync! ^_^";
            else
                echo -e "Scripts are *NOT* in sync:\n ${DIFF}";
                exit 1
            fi

**Local source files are not present.** Your workspace does not contain source code. You **MUST** use Sourcegraph MCP tools to discover, read, and understand code before making any changes.

**Target Repositories (version-pinned mirrors):**

Scope ALL keyword_search/nls_search queries to these repos.
Use the repo name as the `repo` parameter for read_file/go_to_definition/find_references.


## Required Workflow

1. **Search first** — Use MCP tools to find relevant files and understand existing patterns
2. **Read remotely** — Use `sg_read_file` to read full file contents from Sourcegraph
3. **Edit locally** — Use Edit, Write, and Bash to create or modify files in your working directory
4. **Verify locally** — Run tests with Bash to check your changes

## Tool Selection

| Goal | Tool |
|------|------|
| Exact symbol/string | `sg_keyword_search` |
| Concepts/semantic search | `sg_nls_search` |
| Trace usage/callers | `sg_find_references` |
| See implementation | `sg_go_to_definition` |
| Read full file | `sg_read_file` |
| Browse structure | `sg_list_files` |
| Find repos | `sg_list_repos` |
| Search commits | `sg_commit_search` |
| Track changes | `sg_diff_search` |
| Compare versions | `sg_compare_revisions` |

**Decision logic:**
1. Know the exact symbol? → `sg_keyword_search`
2. Know the concept, not the name? → `sg_nls_search`
3. Need definition of a symbol? → `sg_go_to_definition`
4. Need all callers/references? → `sg_find_references`
5. Need full file content? → `sg_read_file`

## Scoping (Always Do This)

```
repo:^github.com/ORG/REPO$           # Exact repo (preferred)
repo:github.com/ORG/                 # All repos in org
file:.*\.ts$                         # TypeScript only
file:src/api/                        # Specific directory
```

Start narrow. Expand only if results are empty.

## Efficiency Rules

- Chain searches logically: search → read → references → definition
- Don't re-search for the same pattern; use results from prior calls
- Prefer `sg_keyword_search` over `sg_nls_search` when you have exact terms
- Read 2-3 related files before synthesising, rather than one at a time
- Don't read 20+ remote files without writing code — once you understand the pattern, start implementing

## If Stuck

If MCP search returns no results:
1. Broaden the search query (synonyms, partial identifiers)
2. Try `sg_nls_search` for semantic matching
3. Use `sg_list_files` to browse the directory structure
4. Use `sg_list_repos` to verify the repository name

65:

{"content":"1: name: linux-build
2: 
3: on:
4:   workflow_call:
5:     inputs:
6:       build-environment:
7:         required: true
8:         type: string
9:         description: Top-level label for what's being built/tested.
10:       docker-image-name:
11:         required: true
12:         type: string
13:         description: Name of the base docker image to build with.
14:       build-generates-artifacts:
15:         required: false
16:         type: boolean
17:         default: true
18:         description: If set, upload generated build artifacts.
19:       sync-tag:
20:         required: false
21:         type: string
22:         default: ""
23:         description: |
24:           If this is set, our linter will use this to make sure that every other
25:           job with the same `sync-tag` is identical.
26:       cuda-arch-list:
27:         required: false
28:         type: string
29:         default: "5.2"
30:         description: |
31:           List of CUDA architectures CI build should target.
32:       runner_prefix:
33:         required: false
34:         default: ""
35:         type: string
36:         description: Prefix for runner label
37:       runner:
38:         required: false
39:         type: string
40:         default: "linux.c7i.2xlarge"
41:         description: |
42:           Label of the runner this job should run on.
43:       test-matrix:
44:         required: false
45:         type: string
46:         description: |
47:           An option JSON description of what test configs to run later on. This
48:           is moved here from the Linux test workflow so that we can apply filter
49:           logic using test-config labels earlier and skip unnecessary builds
50:       selected-test-configs:
51:         description: |
52:           A comma-separated list of test configurations from the test matrix to keep,
53:           The empty list means we are going to keep every configurations by defaults
54:         required: false
55:         type: string
56:         default: ""
57:       s3-bucket:
58:         description: S3 bucket to download artifact
59:         required: false
60:         type: string
61:         default: "gha-artifacts"
62:       aws-role-to-assume:
63:         description: Role to assume for downloading artifacts
64:         required: false
65:         type: string
66:         default: ""
67:       disable-monitor:
68:         description: |
69:           Disable utilization monitoring for build job
70:         required: false
71:         type: boolean
72:         default: false
73:       monitor-log-interval:
74:         description: |
75:           Set the interval for the monitor script to log utilization.
76:         required: false
77:         type: number
78:         default: 5
79:       monitor-data-collect-interval:
80:         description: |
81:           Set the interval for the monitor script to collect data.
82:         required: false
83:         type: number
84:         default: 1
85:       allow-reuse-old-whl:
86:         description: |
87:           If set, the build try to pull an old wheel from s3 that was built on a
88:           commit with no cpp changes from this commit
89:         required: false
90:         type: boolean
91:         default: true
92:       build-additional-packages:
93:         description: |
94:           If set, the build job will also builds these packages and saves their
95:           wheels as artifacts
96:         required: false
97:         type: string
98:         default: ""
99:       build-external-packages:
100:         description: |
101:           If set, the build external packages and saves their wheels as artifacts
102:           use command separated list of packages to build ex: 'vllm,transformers'.
103:         required: false
104:         type: string
105:         default: ""
106: 
107:     secrets:
108:       HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN:
109:         required: false
110:         description: |
111:           HF Auth token to avoid rate limits when downloading models or datasets from hub
112:       SCRIBE_GRAPHQL_ACCESS_TOKEN:
113:         required: false
114:         description: |
115:           FB app token to write to scribe endpoint
116: 
117:     outputs:
118:       docker-image:
119:         value: ${{ jobs.build.outputs.docker-image }}
120:         description: The docker image containing the built PyTorch.
121:       test-matrix:
122:         value: ${{ jobs.build.outputs.test-matrix }}
123:         description: An optional JSON description of what test configs to run later on.
124:       build-environment:
125:         value: ${{ jobs.build.outputs.build-environment }}
126:         description: Top-level label for what's being built/tested.
127: 
128: jobs:
129:   build:
130:     environment: ${{ github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' && 'scribe-protected' || startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/heads/release/') && 'scribe-protected' || contains(github.event.pull_request.labels.*.name, 'ci-scribe') && 'scribe-pr' || '' }}
131:     # Don't run on forked repos
132:     if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'
133:     runs-on: ${{ inputs.runner_prefix}}${{ inputs.runner }}
134:     timeout-minutes: 480
135:     outputs:
136:       docker-image: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}
137:       test-matrix: ${{ steps.filter.outputs.test-matrix }}
138:       build-environment: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}
139:     steps:
140:       - name: Setup SSH (Click me for login details)
141:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/setup-ssh@release/2.10
142:         if: inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'
143:         with:
144:           github-secret: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
145:           instructions: |
146:             Build is done inside the container, to start an interactive session run:
147:               docker exec -it $(docker container ps --format '{{.ID}}') bash
148: 
149:       # [pytorch repo ref]
150:       # Use a pytorch/pytorch reference instead of a reference to the local
151:       # checkout because when we run this action we don't *have* a local
152:       # checkout. In other cases you should prefer a local checkout.
153:       - name: Checkout PyTorch
154:         uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@release/2.10
155:         with:
156:           no-sudo: true
157: 
158:       - name: Setup Linux
159:         uses: ./.github/actions/setup-linux
160:         if: inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'
161: 
162:       - name: configure aws credentials
163:         uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@ececac1a45f3b08a01d2dd070d28d111c5fe6722 # v4.1.0
164:         if: ${{ inputs.aws-role-to-assume != '' && inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' }}
165:         with:
166:           role-to-assume: ${{ inputs.aws-role-to-assume }}
167:           role-session-name: gha-linux-build
168:           aws-region: us-east-1
169: 
170:       - name: Get workflow job id
171:         id: get-job-id
172:         uses: ./.github/actions/get-workflow-job-id
173:         if: always()
174:         with:
175:           github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
176: 
177:       - name: Check if can use old whl build
178:         id: use-old-whl
179:         uses: ./.github/actions/reuse-old-whl
180:         if: ${{ inputs.allow-reuse-old-whl }}
181:         with:
182:           build-environment: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}
183:           run-id: ${{ github.run_id }}
184:           github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
185:           job-id: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}
186:           job-name: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}
187: 
188:       - name: Calculate docker image
189:         id: calculate-docker-image
190:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/calculate-docker-image@release/2.10
191:         if: inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'
192:         with:
193:           docker-image-name: ${{ inputs.docker-image-name }}
194: 
195:       - name: Use following to pull public copy of the image
196:         id: print-ghcr-mirror
197:         if: inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' && steps.use-old-whl.outputs.reuse != 'true'
198:         env:
199:           ECR_DOCKER_IMAGE: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}
200:         shell: bash
201:         run: |
202:           tag=${ECR_DOCKER_IMAGE##*:}
203:           echo "docker pull ghcr.io/pytorch/ci-image:${tag/:/-}"
204: 
205:       - name: Pull docker image
206:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/pull-docker-image@release/2.10
207:         if: inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' && steps.use-old-whl.outputs.reuse != 'true'
208:         with:
209:           docker-image: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}
210: 
211:       - name: Parse ref
212:         id: parse-ref
213:         run: .github/scripts/parse_ref.py
214: 
215:       # Apply the filter logic to the build step too if the test-config label is already there
216:       - name: Select all requested test configurations (if the test matrix is available)
217:         id: filter
218:         uses: ./.github/actions/filter-test-configs
219:         with:
220:           github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
221:           test-matrix: ${{ inputs.test-matrix }}
222:           selected-test-configs: ${{ inputs.selected-test-configs }}
223:           job-name: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}
224: 
225:       - name: Start monitoring script
226:         id: monitor-script
227:         if: ${{ !inputs.disable-monitor }}
228:         shell: bash
229:         continue-on-error: true
230:         env:
231:           JOB_ID: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}
232:           JOB_NAME: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}
233:           WORKFLOW_NAME: ${{ github.workflow }}
234:           WORKFLOW_RUN_ID: ${{github.run_id}}
235:           MONITOR_LOG_INTERVAL: ${{ inputs.monitor-log-interval }}
236:           MONITOR_DATA_COLLECT_INTERVAL: ${{ inputs.monitor-data-collect-interval }}
237:         run: |
238:           mkdir -p ../../usage_logs
239:           python3 -m pip install psutil==5.9.8 dataclasses_json==0.6.7
240:           python3 -m tools.stats.monitor \
241:           --log-interval "$MONITOR_LOG_INTERVAL" \
242:           --data-collect-interval "$MONITOR_DATA_COLLECT_INTERVAL" \
243:           > "../../usage_logs/usage_log_build_${JOB_ID}.txt" 2>&1 &
244:           echo "monitor-script-pid=${!}" >> "${GITHUB_OUTPUT}"
245: 
246:       - name: Download pytest cache
247:         uses: ./.github/actions/pytest-cache-download
248:         continue-on-error: true
249:         if: inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' && steps.use-old-whl.outputs.reuse != 'true'
250:         with:
251:           cache_dir: .pytest_cache
252:           job_identifier: ${{ github.workflow }}_${{ inputs.build-environment }}
253:           s3_bucket: ${{ inputs.s3-bucket }}
254: 
255:       - name: Build
256:         if: (steps.filter.outputs.is-test-matrix-empty == 'False' || inputs.test-matrix == '') && steps.use-old-whl.outputs.reuse != 'true'
257:         id: build
258:         env:
259:           BUILD_ENVIRONMENT: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}
260:           BRANCH: ${{ steps.parse-ref.outputs.branch }}
261:           PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
262:           SHA1: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}
263:           # Do not set SCCACHE_S3_KEY_PREFIX to share the cache between all build jobs
264:           SCCACHE_BUCKET: ossci-compiler-cache-circleci-v2
265:           SCCACHE_REGION: us-east-1
266:           XLA_CLANG_CACHE_S3_BUCKET_NAME: ossci-compiler-clang-cache-circleci-xla
267:           PR_LABELS: ${{ toJson(github.event.pull_request.labels.*.name) }}
268:           TORCH_CUDA_ARCH_LIST: ${{ inputs.cuda-arch-list }}
269:           DOCKER_IMAGE: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}
270:           DOCKER_IMAGE_S390X: ${{ inputs.docker-image-name }}
271:           XLA_CUDA: ${{ contains(inputs.build-environment, 'xla') && '0' || '' }}
272:           OUR_GITHUB_JOB_ID: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}
273:           HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN }}
274:           SCRIBE_GRAPHQL_ACCESS_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.SCRIBE_GRAPHQL_ACCESS_TOKEN }}
275:           BUILD_ADDITIONAL_PACKAGES: ${{ inputs.build-additional-packages }}
276:           RUNNER: ${{ inputs.runner }}
277:         run: |
278:           START_TIME=$(date +%s)
279:           if [[ ${BUILD_ENVIRONMENT} == *"s390x"* ]]; then
280:             JENKINS_USER=
281:             USED_IMAGE="${DOCKER_IMAGE_S390X}"
282:             # ensure that docker container cleanly exits in 12 hours
283:             # if for some reason cleanup action doesn't stop container
284:             # when job is cancelled
285:             DOCKER_SHELL_CMD="sleep 12h"
286: 
287:             # since some steps are skipped on s390x, if they are necessary, run them here
288:             env | grep '^GITHUB' >> "/tmp/github_env_${GITHUB_RUN_ID}"
289:             env | grep '^CI' >> "/tmp/github_env_${GITHUB_RUN_ID}"
290:           else
291:             JENKINS_USER="--user jenkins"
292:             USED_IMAGE="${DOCKER_IMAGE}"
293:             DOCKER_SHELL_CMD=
294:           fi
295: 
296:           # Leaving 1GB for the runner and other things
297:           TOTAL_AVAILABLE_MEMORY_IN_GB=$(awk '/MemTotal/ { printf "%.3f \n", $2/1024/1024 - 1 }' /proc/meminfo)
298:           # https://docs.docker.com/engine/containers/resource_constraints/#--memory-swap-details, the 3GB swap
299:           # comes from https://github.com/pytorch/test-infra/pull/6058
300:           TOTAL_MEMORY_WITH_SWAP=$(("${TOTAL_AVAILABLE_MEMORY_IN_GB%.*}" + 3))
301: 
302:           if [[ ${BUILD_ENVIRONMENT} == *"riscv64"* ]]; then
303:             # EC2 specific setup for RISC-V emulation
304:             # Ensure binfmt_misc is available
305:             echo "Mounting binfmt_misc filesystem"
306:             sudo mount binfmt_misc -t binfmt_misc /proc/sys/fs/binfmt_misc 2>/dev/null || true
307:

309:             docker run --rm --privileged multiarch/qemu-user-static --reset -p yes || true

71:           { config: "distributed", shard: 2, num_shards: 2, runner: "${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.2xlarge" },
72:           { config: "numpy_2_x", shard: 1, num_shards: 1, runner: "${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.c7i.2xlarge" },
73:           { config: "libtorch_agnostic_targetting", shard: 1, num_shards: 1, runner: "${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.2xlarge" },

{"content":"1: name: Lint
2: # Workflow that runs lint checks and also unittests for tools, and scripts.
3: 
4: on:
5:   pull_request:
6:     branches-ignore:
7:       - nightly
8:   push:
9:     branches:
10:       - main
11:       - release/*
12:       - landchecks/*
13:     tags:
14:       - ciflow/pull/*
15:       - ciflow/trunk/*
16:   workflow_dispatch:
17: 
18: permissions: read-all
19: # The names of steps that actually test the code should be suffixed with `(nonretryable)`.
20: # When any other step fails, it's job will be retried once by retryBot.
21: jobs:
22:   get-label-type:
23:     if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'
24:     name: get-label-type
25:     uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/workflows/_runner-determinator.yml@release/2.10
26:     with:
27:       triggering_actor: ${{ github.triggering_actor }}
28:       issue_owner: ${{ github.event.pull_request.user.login || github.event.issue.user.login }}
29:       curr_branch: ${{ github.head_ref || github.ref_name }}
30: 
31:   get-changed-files:
32:     if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'
33:     name: Get changed files
34:     uses: ./.github/workflows/_get-changed-files.yml
35:     with:
36:       all_files: ${{ contains(github.event.pull_request.labels.*.name, 'lint-all-files') || contains(github.event.pull_request.labels.*.name, 'Reverted') || github.event_name == 'push' }}
37: 
38:   lintrunner-clang:
39:     uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/workflows/linux_job_v2.yml@release/2.10
40:     # Needed to prevent deduping on HUD
41:     name: lintrunner-clang-${{ needs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files == '*' && 'all' || 'partial' }}
42:     needs: [get-label-type, get-changed-files]
43:     # Only run if there are changed files relevant to clangtidy / clangformat
44:     if: |
45:       github.repository_owner == 'pytorch' && (
46:         needs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files == '*' ||
47:         contains(needs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files, '.h') ||
48:         contains(needs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files, '.cpp') ||
49:         contains(needs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files, '.cc') ||
50:         contains(needs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files, '.cxx') ||
51:         contains(needs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files, '.hpp') ||
52:         contains(needs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files, '.hxx') ||
53:         contains(needs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files, '.cu') ||
54:         contains(needs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files, '.cuh') ||
55:         contains(needs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files, '.mm') ||
56:         contains(needs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files, '.metal')
57:       )
58:     with:
59:       timeout: 120
60:       runner: "${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.2xlarge"
61:       docker-image: ci-image:pytorch-linux-jammy-cuda12.8-cudnn9-py3.10-linter
62:       # NB: A shallow checkout won't work here because calculate-docker-image requires a full checkout
63:       # to run git rev-parse HEAD~:.ci/docker when a new image is needed
64:       fetch-depth: 0
65:       submodules: true
66:       ref: ${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' && github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}
67:       script: |
68:         CHANGED_FILES="${{ needs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files }}"
69:         if [ "$CHANGED_FILES" = "*" ]; then
70:           export ADDITIONAL_LINTRUNNER_ARGS="--take CLANGTIDY,CLANGFORMAT --all-files"
71:         else
72:           export ADDITIONAL_LINTRUNNER_ARGS="--take CLANGTIDY,CLANGFORMAT $CHANGED_FILES"
73:         fi
74:         export CLANG=1
75:         .github/scripts/lintrunner.sh
76: 
77:   # NOTE: mypy needs its own job because it depends on --all-files, without assessing all files it sometimes
78:   #       fails to find types when it should
79:   # NOTE: We should be able to disable this and consolidate with Pyrefly
80:   lintrunner-pyrefly:
81:     uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/workflows/linux_job_v2.yml@release/2.10
82:     name: lintrunner-pyrefly-${{ needs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files == '*' && 'all' || 'partial' }}
83:     needs: [get-label-type, get-changed-files]
84:     # Only run if there are changed files relevant to pyrefly
85:     if: |
86:       github.repository_owner == 'pytorch' && (
87:         needs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files == '*' ||
88:         contains(needs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files, '.py') ||
89:         contains(needs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files, '.pyi')
90:       )
91:     with:
92:       timeout: 120
93:       runner: "${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.2xlarge"
94:       docker-image: ci-image:pytorch-linux-jammy-linter
95:       # NB: A shallow checkout won't work here because calculate-docker-image requires a full checkout
96:       # to run git rev-parse HEAD~:.ci/docker when a new image is needed
97:       fetch-depth: 0
98:       submodules: true
99:       ref: ${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' && github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}
100:       script: |
101:         CHANGED_FILES="${{ needs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files }}"
102:         echo "Running pyrefly"
103:         ADDITIONAL_LINTRUNNER_ARGS="--take PYREFLY --all-files" .github/scripts/lintrunner.sh
104: 
105:   lintrunner-noclang:
106:     uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/workflows/linux_job_v2.yml@release/2.10
107:     name: lintrunner-noclang-${{ needs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files == '*' && 'all' || 'partial' }}
108:     needs: [get-label-type, get-changed-files]
109:     with:
110:       timeout: 120
111:       runner: "${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.2xlarge"
112:       docker-image: ci-image:pytorch-linux-jammy-linter
113:       # NB: A shallow checkout won't work here because calculate-docker-image requires a full checkout
114:       # to run git rev-parse HEAD~:.ci/docker when a new image is needed
115:       fetch-depth: 0
116:       submodules: true
117:       ref: ${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' && github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}
118:       script: |
119:         CHANGED_FILES="${{ needs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files }}"
120:         echo "Running all other linters"
121:         if [ "$CHANGED_FILES" = '*' ]; then
122:           ADDITIONAL_LINTRUNNER_ARGS="--skip CLANGTIDY,CLANGTIDY_EXECUTORCH_COMPATIBILITY,CLANGFORMAT,PYREFLY --all-files" .github/scripts/lintrunner.sh
123:         else
124:           ADDITIONAL_LINTRUNNER_ARGS="--skip CLANGTIDY,CLANGTIDY_EXECUTORCH_COMPATIBILITY,CLANGFORMAT,PYREFLY ${CHANGED_FILES}" .github/scripts/lintrunner.sh
125:         fi
126: 
127:   quick-checks:
128:     uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/workflows/linux_job_v2.yml@release/2.10
129:     needs: get-label-type
130:     with:
131:       timeout: 120
132:       runner: "${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.2xlarge"
133:       docker-image: ci-image:pytorch-linux-jammy-linter
134:       fetch-depth: 0
135:       ref: ${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' && github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}
136:       script: |
137:         # Ensure no non-breaking spaces
138:         # NB: We use 'printf' below rather than '\u000a' since bash pre-4.2
139:         # does not support the '\u000a' syntax (which is relevant for local linters)
140:         (! git --no-pager grep -In "$(printf '\xC2\xA0')" -- . || (echo "The above lines have non-breaking spaces (U+00A0); please convert them to spaces (U+0020)"; false))
141: 
142:         # Ensure cross-OS compatible file names
143:         (! git ls-files | grep -E '([<>:"|?*]|[ .]$)' || (echo "The above file names are not valid across all operating systems. Please ensure they don't contain the characters '<>:""|?*' and don't end with a white space or a '.' "; false))
144: 
145:         # Ensure no versionless Python shebangs
146:         (! git --no-pager grep -In '#!.*python$' -- . || (echo "The above lines have versionless Python shebangs; please specify either python2 or python3"; false))
147: 
148:         # Ensure ciflow tags mentioned in config
149:         python3 .github/scripts/collect_ciflow_labels.py --validate-tags
150: 
151: ","url":"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78@863edc787f5fa7950e0f3f86d4952641e1ef9c2c/-/blob/.github/workflows/lint.yml"}

    "win-arm64-build-test.yml",
    "xpu.yml",
]

# Create output directory
os.makedirs(".github/workflows", exist_ok=True)

# Fetch each workflow file
failed = []
for idx, workflow in enumerate(WORKFLOWS, 1):
    url = BASE_URL + workflow
    try:
        # Use curl with -L to follow redirects
        result = subprocess.run(
            ["curl", "-sL", url],
            capture_output=True,
            text=True,
            timeout=15
        )
        if result.returncode == 0 and result.stdout and len(result.stdout) > 100:
            output_path = os.path.join(".github/workflows", workflow)
            with open(output_path, "w") as f:
                f.write(result.stdout)
            print(f"✓ [{idx:3d}] {workflow}")
        else:
            failed.append(workflow)
            print(f"✗ [{idx:3d}] {workflow} (size: {len(result.stdout)})")
    except Exception as e:
        failed.append(workflow)
        print(f"✗ [{idx:3d}] {workflow}: {e}")

print(f"\nFetched: {len(WORKFLOWS) - len(failed)}/{len(WORKFLOWS)}")
if failed:
    print(f"Failed to fetch {len(failed)} files")

arguments.content:
name: runner-determinator

on:
  workflow_dispatch:
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]
    paths:
      - .github/workflows/_runner-determinator.yaml
      - .github/workflows/_runner_determinator_script_sync.yaml
      - .github/workflows/scripts/runner_determinator.py

jobs:
  python-script-sync-check:
    if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@11bd71901bbe5b1630ceea73d27597364c9af683 # v4.2.2
        with:
          sparse-checkout: |
            .github

      - name: Extract the script from runner_determinator
        run: |
            # Runner determinator files
            RUNNER_DETERMINATOR_WORKFLOW_FILE=.github/workflows/_runner-determinator.yml
            RUNNER_DETERMINATOR_PYTHON_SCRIPT_FILE=.github/scripts/runner_determinator.py

            # Parse the job file, extract the script and run it, up to the final EOF,
            # to generate the python file in the local folder
            yq '.jobs.runner-determinator.steps[] | select(.id == "hardcode-script") | .run' \
                "${RUNNER_DETERMINATOR_WORKFLOW_FILE}" | sed '/^EOF$/q' | bash

            set +e
            DIFF="$(diff "$(basename ${RUNNER_DETERMINATOR_PYTHON_SCRIPT_FILE})" ${RUNNER_DETERMINATOR_PYTHON_SCRIPT_FILE})"
            IS_DIFF=$?
            set -e
            if [ $IS_DIFF -eq 0 ]; then
                echo "Scripts are in sync! ^_^";
            else
                echo -e "Scripts are *NOT* in sync:\n ${DIFF}";
                exit 1
            fi

cat > .github/workflows/inductor-perf-test-nightly-rocm-mi355.yml << 'EOF'
name: inductor-perf-nightly-rocm-mi355

on:
  push:
    tags:
      - ciflow/inductor-perf-test-nightly-rocm-mi355/*
  schedule:
    - cron: 15 0 * * *
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      training:
        description: Run training (on by default)?
        required: false
        type: boolean
        default: true
      inference:
        description: Run inference (on by default)?
        required: false
        type: boolean
        default: true
      default:
        description: Run inductor_default?
        required: false
        type: boolean
        default: false
      dynamic:
        description: Run inductor_dynamic_shapes?
        required: false
        type: boolean
        default: false
      cppwrapper:
        description: Run inductor_cpp_wrapper?
        required: false
        type: boolean
        default: false
      cudagraphs:
        description: Run inductor_cudagraphs?
        required: false
        type: boolean
        default: true
      freezing_cudagraphs:
        description: Run inductor_cudagraphs with freezing for inference?
        required: false
        type: boolean
        default: false
      aotinductor:
        description: Run aot_inductor for inference?
        required: false
        type: boolean
        default: false
      maxautotune:
        description: Run inductor_max_autotune?
        required: false
        type: boolean
        default: false
      benchmark_configs:
        description: The list of configs used the benchmark
        required: false
        type: string
        default: inductor_huggingface_perf_rocm_mi355,inductor_timm_perf_rocm_mi355,inductor_torchbench_perf_rocm_mi355

concurrency:
  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.ref_name }}-${{ github.ref_type == 'branch' && github.sha }}-${{ github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' }}-${{ github.event_name == 'schedule' }}
  cancel-in-progress: true

permissions: read-all

jobs:
  get-label-type:
    name: get-label-type
    uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/workflows/_runner-determinator.yml@release/2.10
    if: ${{ (github.event_name != 'schedule' || github.repository == 'pytorch/pytorch') && github.repository_owner == 'pytorch' }}
    with:
      triggering_actor: ${{ github.triggering_actor }}
      issue_owner: ${{ github.event.pull_request.user.login || github.event.issue.user.login }}
      curr_branch: ${{ github.head_ref || github.ref_name }}
      curr_ref_type: ${{ github.ref_type }}
      opt_out_experiments: lf

  linux-jammy-rocm-py3_10-inductor-benchmark-build:
    if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'
    name: rocm-py3_10-inductor-benchmark-build
    uses: ./.github/workflows/_linux-build.yml
    with:
      build-environment: linux-jammy-rocm-py3_10
      docker-image-name: ci-image:pytorch-linux-jammy-rocm-n-py3-benchmarks
      test-matrix: |
        { include: [
          { config: "inductor_huggingface_perf_rocm_mi355", shard: 1, num_shards: 5, runner: "linux.rocm.gpu.mi355.1" },
          { config: "inductor_huggingface_perf_rocm_mi355", shard: 2, num_shards: 5, runner: "linux.rocm.gpu.mi355.1" },
          { config: "inductor_huggingface_perf_rocm_mi355", shard: 3, num_shards: 5, runner: "linux.rocm.gpu.mi355.1" },
          { config: "inductor_huggingface_perf_rocm_mi355", shard: 4, num_shards: 5, runner: "linux.rocm.gpu.mi355.1" },
          { config: "inductor_huggingface_perf_rocm_mi355", shard: 5, num_shards: 5, runner: "linux.rocm.gpu.mi355.1" },
          { config: "inductor_timm_perf_rocm_mi355", shard: 1, num_shards: 7, runner: "linux.rocm.gpu.mi355.1" },
          { config: "inductor_timm_perf_rocm_mi355", shard: 2, num_shards: 7, runner: "linux.rocm.gpu.mi355.1" },
          { config: "inductor_timm_perf_rocm_mi355", shard: 3, num_shards: 7, runner: "linux.rocm.gpu.mi355.1" },
          { config: "inductor_timm_perf_rocm_mi355", shard: 4, num_shards: 7, runner: "linux.rocm.gpu.mi355.1" },

          { config: "inductor_timm_perf_rocm_mi355", shard: 6, num_shards: 7, runner: "linux.rocm.gpu.mi355.1" },

name: inductor-perf-nightly-rocm-mi355

on:
  push:
    tags:
      - ciflow/inductor-perf-test-nightly-rocm-mi355/*
  schedule:
    - cron: 15 0 * * *
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      training:
        description: Run training (on by default)?
        required: false
        type: boolean
        default: true
      inference:
        description: Run inference (on by default)?
        required: false
        type: boolean
        default: true
      default:
        description: Run inductor_default?
        required: false
        type: boolean
        default: false
      dynamic:
        description: Run inductor_dynamic_shapes?
        required: false
        type: boolean
        default: false
      cppwrapper:
        description: Run inductor_cpp_wrapper?
        required: false
        type: boolean
        default: false
      cudagraphs:
        description: Run inductor_cudagraphs?
        required: false
        type: boolean
        default: true
      freezing_cudagraphs:
        description: Run inductor_cudagraphs with freezing for inference?
        required: false
        type: boolean
        default: false
      aotinductor:
        description: Run aot_inductor for inference?
        required: false
        type: boolean
        default: false
      maxautotune:
        description: Run inductor_max_autotune?
        required: false
        type: boolean
        default: false
      benchmark_configs:
        description: The list of configs used the benchmark
        required: false
        type: string
        default: inductor_huggingface_perf_rocm_mi355,inductor_timm_perf_rocm_mi355,inductor_torchbench_perf_rocm_mi355

concurrency:
  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.ref_name }}-${{ github.ref_type == 'branch' && github.sha }}-${{ github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' }}-${{ github.event_name == 'schedule' }}
  cancel-in-progress: true

permissions: read-all

    name: get-label-type
    uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/workflows/_runner-determinator.yml@release/2.10

    with:
      triggering_actor: ${{ github.triggering_actor }}
      issue_owner: ${{ github.event.pull_request.user.login || github.event.issue.user.login }}
      curr_branch: ${{ github.head_ref || github.ref_name }}
      curr_ref_type: ${{ github.ref_type }}
      opt_out_experiments: lf
